name: @chbrain/khai-engine-self-efficacy
description: khai engine content, the self-efficacy domain: self-efficacy as position, the persona's task-specific belief in their own capability to organize and execute the actions a given attainment requires -- built from mastery, vicarious experience, and verbal persuasion.
license: SEE LICENSE IN LICENSE and LICENSE-CODE

khai.tagline: Self-efficacy as position: the persona's task-specific belief in their own capability to organize and execute the actions a given attainment requires -- built and eroded by mastery, vicarious example, and persuasion, never a verdict on global self-worth.

card.wire: Attaches at Position. The anchor (position_self_efficacy.md) declares self-efficacy as a task-specific belief in one's own capability to execute -- not a global verdict on worth -- and the system-wide law makes every persona carry a read on their own capability beneath every plan.
card.issue: Three ready expressions naming where a persona's efficacy read was built: mastery (grounded in one's own remembered successes, the strongest and most durable source), vicarious (read off watching a similar other succeed or fail, borrowed rather than earned), and persuasion (lent by another's credible word, the most fragile source, apt to fold at the first setback). Each is a full position file the build composes onto the anchor, so the persona gets a capability belief coloured by its source, not a bare label. More expressions (e.g. a physiological-state source) slot in as sibling files without moving the anchor.
card.require: Two altitudes must hold. One, the law: declared once in the project's Instructions Knowledge chapter ('personas carry a self-efficacy'), so the capability read holds system-wide for whatever task is at hand. Two, the link: each persona carries its self-efficacy under Projection. A persona may also hold a different, even contradicting, read under Shadow; the gap between the confidence shown and the doubt masked is the character, not a contradiction to reject.
card.enforce: Structure is checked; coherence is judged. The kit verifies the floor: the law is declared, and each persona carries a self-efficacy under Projection. Whether a Projection read and a Shadow read cohere into a true persona is semantic, so it is taught and reviewed, never failed by the suite. The engine ships its own structural tests; the reading is guided, not gated.
card.setup: Two steps. Declare the law in the Instructions Knowledge chapter, linking the anchor. Then give each persona its self-efficacy under Projection: one expression, naming the source it was built from. Where a persona masks a different read, put it under Shadow. Read it in how readily the persona commits to the task at hand, never narrated as a global claim about their worth.
